key_insight: PINN-based, solver-agnostic training can learn turbulence closures that generalize across bluff-body shapes without embedding a CFD solver in the optimization loop.
engineering_relevance: This offers a faster, more portable way to develop and screen RANS closures for practical flow prediction, including cases where DNS is unavailable and only sparse PIV data exist.
method: Train closures inside a PINN by enforcing the RANS residual via automatic differentiation, avoiding mesh dependence and solver adjoints.
Test four closure families: three Reynolds-stress tensor models with realizability constraints and one direct force model for \u2212\u2207\u22c5\u03c4.
Improve stability with input-gradient smoothing and a Lipschitz constraint before deploying the frozen closures in a finite-element solver.
evidence_signal: The models were trained on six 2D bluff-body wakes at Re = 10^4 and evaluated with in-sample tests plus strict leave-one-shape-out validation; the force model achieved the best out-of-sample performance, with about 8.5% LOSO drag error.
watch_item: Although the approach is solver-agnostic at training time, the learned closure still must remain stable when coupled to a downstream RANS solver and may be sensitive to the chosen stability regularization.
paper_title: Generalizable turbulence closures across bluff-body shapes by PINN-based solver-agnostic training
